free_text: Single particle effects in precompound reactions
Faraday cup.
Neutron time of flight.
11.4 cm diameter x 5.1 cm NE213 scintillators.
Detector efficiencies calculated with code EFFIC.
After background correction the time-of-flight spectra
were converted into center-of-mass energy spectra
under the assumption of single nucleon emission.
Corrected for background and isotopic impurities.
The rare events of neutrons following alpha emission
are neglected.
The main systematic uncertainties sum up 9% and arise
from:
. neutron detector efficiency (7%),
. effective target thickness due to inhomogeneities and
uncertainties of the energy-range tables (5%),
. incomplete beam current integration (3%),
To this number the uncertainty in background
subtraction less or equal 5%, but higher at 3.5 degree
and statistical uncertainties that depend on target,
angle, neutron energy have to be added.
Uncertainty in energy.
Neutron energy errors for neutron actually stemming
from second chance emission are at most equal to the
recoil correction for the highest possible nucleon
energy allowing for secondary emission of neutrons
with energies above the detector threshold.
Approved by W. Scobel, 7 August 2002.
VM+CAJaD.
BIB correction
Self-supporting metallic foils of 2.38 cm diameter and
thickness 5.3 mg/cm**2.  Isotopic composition
Staistical uncertainty given.
Overall uncertainties range from 11% in the evaporation
region and for the forward hemisphere to 30% or more
for forward angles and higher energies.
Data given in tables in UCID-20101 (tables not clear).
First data point for each angle added.
